name: Eugene Ormandy
abstract: Eugene Ormandy (born Jenő Blau; November 18, 1899 – March 12, 1985) was a Hungarian-born American conductor and violinist, best known for his association with the Philadelphia Orchestra, as its music director. His 44-year association with the orchestra is one of the longest enjoyed by any conductor with any American orchestra.

about_quotes.speaker: Byron Stripling
about_quotes.evidence: Seiji Ozawa, he’s going to sound different than if Eugene Ormandy, Eugene, just his presence is going to give — the people know they have to play differently for each person.
about_quotes.doc_title: Byron Stripling interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 25, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: he and I used to talk a lot about that... When he passed away I was invited to conduct his orchestra.
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: Alan has played for most of the major names in the classical and jazz world, including Eugene Ormandy
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Chauncey Morehouse
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Do you remember playing under Eugene Ormandy? [INTERVIEWEE] No.
